content.problemStatement:
**Problem Statement: ** We are given an ‘N*M’ matrix. Every cell of the matrix has some chocolates
    on it, mat[i][j] gives us the number of chocolates. We have two friends ‘Alice’ and ‘Bob’. initially, Alice is
    standing on the cell(0,0) and Bob is standing on the cell(0, M-1). Both of them can move only to the cells below
    them in these three directions: to the bottom cell (↓), to the bottom-right cell(↘), or to the bottom-left cell(↙).

    When Alica and Bob visit a cell, they take all the chocolates from that cell with them. It can happen that they
    visit the same cell, in that case, the chocolates need to be considered only once.

    They cannot go out of the boundary of the given matrix, we need to return the maximum number of c
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers N and M, representing the number of rows and columns in the grid. The next N lines contain M integers each, representing the number of chocolates in each cell.
content.outputFormat: The maximum number of chocolates that can be collected.

solution.approach: Use memoization to store the maximum number of chocolates that can be collected for each subproblem.
